description: Admire the Bund's architectural complex of international styles and the modern skyline of Lujiazui from the water.
A trendy and artistic district filled with boutique shops and creative spaces.
Formerly the China Pavilion of the World Expo, now the Shanghai Art Museum, showcasing masterpieces of contemporary Chinese art.
An innovative commercial space combining shopping and art, regularly hosting contemporary art exhibitions.
A comprehensive museum themed on natural history, highly interactive and suitable for family education.
The east bank promenade of the Huangpu River, the best spot to enjoy views of the Bund's architectural complex.

attraction_name: St. Ignatius Cathedral of Shanghai (Xujiahui Catholic Church)
description: Shanghai's largest Catholic church, a Gothic architectural masterpiece with stunning night lighting.
A world-class art museum in a prime riverside location, hosting international top-tier art exhibitions and an exemplar of modern architecture.
A 494-meter-high observation deck with a unique open design, a core building in the Lujiazui financial district.
China's tallest building, featuring a 118th-floor observation deck offering a 360-degree panoramic view of the city.
The west bank ecological and cultural art belt of the Huangpu River, blending natural scenery with modern art installations, ideal for leisurely strolls.
An international art museum in collaboration with the Centre Pompidou, offering a rich array of modern art exhibitions.
Shanghai's largest natural ecological park, featuring pleasant scenery throughout the seasons, perfect for relaxation.
A modern commercial complex gathering international brands and fine dining, with beautiful night views.
An artistic and creative park featuring galleries, craft shops, and unique cafés.
A museum of ancient Chinese art, housing a rich collection of bronzes, ceramics, calligraphy, and paintings.
A landmark building in Shanghai, an exemplar of French architecture, and a popular social media check-in spot.
